passages.title: Bovids
passages.source_html:
Bovids


A


The family of mammals called bovids belongs to the Artiodactyl class, which also includes giraffes. Bovids are highly diverse group consisting of 137 species, some of which are man's most important domestic animals.


B


Bovids are well represented in most parts of Eurasia and Southeast Asian islands, but they are by far the most numerous and diverse in the latter. Some species of bovid are solitary, but others live in large groups with complex social structures Although bovids have adapted to a wide range of habitats, from arctic tundra to deep tropical forest, the majority of species favour open grassland, scrub or desert. This diversity of habitat is also matched by great diversity in size and form: at one extreme is the royal antelope of West Africa, which stands a mere 25 cm at the shoulder; at the other, the massively built bisons of North America and Europe, growing to a shoulder height of 2.2m.


C


Despite differences in size and appearance, bovids are united by the possession of certain common features. All species are ruminants, which means that they retain undigested food in their stomachs, and regurgitate it as necessary. Bovids are almost exclusively herbivorous*. Typically their teeth are highly modified for browsing and grazing: grass or foliage is cropped with the upper lip and lower incisors** (the upper incisors are usually absent), and then ground down by the cheekteeth. As well as having cloven, or split, hooves, the males of all bovid species and the females of most carry horns. Bovid horns have bony cores covered in a sheath of horny material that is constantly renewed from within; they are unbranched and never shed. They vary in shape and size: the relatively simple horns of a large Indian buffalo may measure around 4m from tip to tip along the outer curve, while the various gazelles have horns with a variety of elegant curves.


D


Five groups, or sub-families, may be distinguished: Bovinae, Antelope, Caprinae, Cephalophinae and Antilocapridae. The sub-family Bovinae comprises most of the larger bovids, including the African bongo, and nilgae, eland, bison and cattle. Unlike most other bovids they are all non-territorial. The ancestors of the various species of domestic cattle banteng, gaur, yak and water buffalo are generally rare and endangered in the wild, while the auroch (the ancestor of the domestic cattle of Europe) is extinct.


E 


The term 'antelope' is not a very precise zoological name – it is used to loosely describe a number of bovids that have followed different lines of development. Antelopes are typically long-legged, fast-running species, often with long horns that may be laid along the back when the animal is in full flight. There are two main sub-groups of antelope: Hippotraginae, which includes the oryx and the addax, and Antilopinae, which generally contains slighter and more graceful animals such as gazelle and the springbok. Antelopes are mainly grassland species, but many have adapted to flooded grasslands: pukus, waterbucks and lechwes are all good at swimming, usually feeding in deepwater, while the sitatunga has long, splayed hooves that enable it to walk freely on swampy ground.


F


The sub-family Caprinae includes the sheep and the goat, together with various relatives such as the goral and the tahr. Most are woolly or have long hair. Several species, such as wild goats, chamois and ibex, are agile cliff- and mountain-dwellers. Tolerance of extreme conditions is most marked in this group: Barbary and bighorn sheep have adapted to arid deserts, while Rocky Mountain sheep survive high up in mountains and musk oxen in arctic tundra. 


G


The duiker of Africa belongs to the Cephalophinae sub-family. It is generally small and solitary, often living in thick forest. Although mainly feeding on grass and leaves, some duikers – unlike most other bovids – are believed to eat insects and feed on dead animal carcasses, and even to kill small animals.


H


The pronghorn is the sole survivor of a New World sub-family of herbivorous ruminants, the Antilocapridae in North America. It is similar in appearance and habits to the Old World antelope. Although greatly reduced in numbers since the arrival of Europeans, and the subsequent enclosure of grasslands, the pronghorn is still found inconsiderable numbers throughout North America, from Washington State to Mexico. When alarmed by the approach of wolves or other predators, hairs on the pronghorn's rump stand erect, so showing and emphasising the white patch there. At this signal, the whole herd gallops off at speed of over 60 km per hour.


*herbivorous: plant-eating

**incisors: front teeth

groups.title: Questions 1-13
groups.source_html:
Questions 1 - 3


Choose the correct answer.

1

In which region is the biggest range of bovids to be found?

正确答案： D. South-east Asia

定位词: region，biggest range of


对应原文：B段，Bovids are well represented in most parts of Eurasia and Southeast Asian islands, but they are by far the most numerous and diverse in the latter.


详细解析:B段指出，东南亚是牛科动物种类最多和最为多样的地区。虽然文章指出牛科动物在非洲也有分布，但并没有指出是最为多样或最为丰富的地区。欧亚大陆但并没有被描述为牛科动物最为多样的地区，并且北美的牛科动物种类并不是最多的。


同义替换：biggest range of bovids = most numerous and diverse


2

Most bovids have a preference for living in

正确答案： D. wide open spaces.

定位词: most bovids,preference


对应原文:B段，Although bovids have adapted to a wide range of habitats, from arctic tundra to deep tropical forest, the majority of species favour open grassland, scrub or desert.


详细解析:B段指出大多数牛科动物更偏好生活在“open grassland, scrub or desert”（开阔的草地、灌木丛或沙漠）。文章没有指出牛科动物偏好孤立的环境， 文章没有指出牛科动物偏好小群体，而是指出它们适应不同的栖息地。虽然有些牛科动物生活在热带森林，但文章指出大多数物种更喜欢开阔的空间。


同义替换：preference for living in = favour，wide open spaces = open grassland, scrub or desert

3

Which of the following features do all bovids have in common?

正确答案： C. They store food in the body.

定位词: all bovids, features...in common


原文部分:C段，All species are ruminants, which means that they retain undigested food in their stomachs, and regurgitate it as necessary.


详细解析:C段指出所有牛科动物都有共同的特点：它们是反刍动物，即它们将未消化的食物保留在胃中并根据需要重新吞咽。原文指出牛科动物的角从未脱落，大多数牛科动物没有上门牙，只有下门牙，并且牛科动物的蹄是分裂的（cloven hooves）。


同义替换：store= retain

Questions 4 - 8


Choose the correct sub-family (A - D) for each characteristic. You may choose any correct sub-family more than once.


List of sub-families

A Antelope

B Bovinae

C Caprinae

D Cephalophinae


Question / ParagraphABCDEFG
4

4 can endure very harsh environments 
✅ 正确答案：C

定位词: Caprinae


对应原文：F段，Tolerance of extreme conditions is most marked in this group: Barbary and bighorn sheep have adapted to arid deserts, while Rocky Mountain sheep survive high up in mountains and musk oxen in arctic tundra.


详细解析:F段指出，Caprinae亚科（包括羊和山羊）能够适应极端环境，如干旱的沙漠、高山和北极苔原。


同义替换：endure=tolerance； harsh environments = extreme conditions
5

5 includes the ox and the cow
✅ 正确答案：B

定位词: Bovinae


对应原文：D段，The sub-family Bovinae comprises most of the larger bovids, including the African bongo, and nilgae, eland, bison and cattle.


详细解析:D段指出，Bovinae亚科包括了较大的牛科物种，如黄牛和水牛（ox and cow）。


同义替换：

includes = comprises
6

6 may supplement its diet with meat
✅ 正确答案：D

定位词:Cephalophinae


对应原文: G段，The duiker of Africa belongs to the Cephalophinae sub-family. It is generally small and solitary, often living in thick forest. Although mainly feeding on grass and leaves, some duikers – unlike most other bovids – are believed to eat insects and feed on dead animal carcasses, and even to kill small animals.


详细解析: G段指出，duiker（杜鹃羚羊）除了吃草和叶子外，还可能会吃昆虫、尸体，甚至小型动物。


同义替换：supplement its diet with= feed on； meat=animal carcasses
7

7 can usually move at speed
✅ 正确答案：A

定位词: Antelope


对应原文：E段，Antelopes are typically long-legged, fast-running species, often with long horns that may be laid along the back when the animal is in full flight.


详细解析: E段指出，Antelope（羚羊）是典型的长腿快速奔跑物种，

，牛类物种通常不以快速奔跑为特点。虽然一些山羊类物种较灵活，但不是特别擅长奔跑。叉角羚虽然奔跑速度较快，但主要指的是羚羊类物种。


同义替换：move at speed = fast-running
8

8 does not defend a particular area of land 
✅ 正确答案：B

定位词: Bovinae


对应原文:D段，The sub-family Bovinae comprises most of the larger bovids, including the African bongo, and nilgae, eland, bison and cattle. Unlike most other bovids they are all non-territorial.


详细解析:D段指出，Bovinae亚科的物种（如黄牛和水牛）通常不防守领土。很多羚羊物种有领土意识，叉角羚有时也会防守领土。


同义替换：does not defend a particular area = non-territorial

Questions 9 - 13


Answer the questions. Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS in each gap.

9

What is the smallest species of Bovid called?

正确答案： 9 the royal antelope##royal antelope
Question 9

定位词: smallest species of Bovid


对应原文:B段，This diversity of habitat is also matched by great diversity in size and form: at one extreme is the royal antelope of West Africa, which stands a mere 25 cm at the shoulder; at the other, the massively built bisons of North America and Europe, growing to a shoulder height of 2.2m.


详细解析:B段指出，royal antelope（王羚）是最小的牛科物种，肩高仅为25厘米。

10

Which species of Bovinae has now died out?

正确答案： 10 the auroch##auroch
Question 10

定位词: died out


对应原文：D段，The ancestors of the various species of domestic cattle banteng, gaur, yak and water buffalo are generally rare and endangered in the wild, while the auroch (the ancestor of the domestic cattle of Europe) is extinct.


详细解析:D段指出，auroch（野牛）已经灭绝，它是欧洲家牛的祖先。


同义替换：died out = is extinct


11

What facilitates the movement of the sitatunga over wetland?

正确答案： 11 long, splayed hooves##splayed hooves##hooves
Question 11

定位词: movement of the sitatunga over wetland


对应原文:E段，Antelopes are mainly grassland species, but many have adapted to flooded grasslands: pukus, waterbucks and lechwes are all good at swimming, usually feeding in deepwater, while the sitatunga has long, splayed hooves that enable it to walk freely on swampy ground.


详细解析:E段指出，sitatunga（西塔图甘羚）拥有长且宽的蹄子，可以帮助它在湿地上自由行走。


同义替换：facilitates the movement = enable it to walk freely

12

What sort of terrain do barbary sheep live in?

正确答案： 12 arid deserts
Question 12

定位词: Barbary sheep live in


对应原文：F段，Tolerance of extreme conditions is most marked in this group: Barbary and bighorn sheep have adapted to arid deserts, while Rocky Mountain sheep survive high up in mountains and musk oxen in arctic tundra.


详细解析:F段指出，Barbary sheep（巴巴里羊）适应了沙漠环境。

13

What is the only living member of the Antilocapridae sub-family?

正确答案： 13 the pronghorn##pronghorn
Question 13

定位词: only living member , Antilocapridae sub-family


对应原文：H段，The pronghorn is the sole survivor of a New World sub-family of herbivorous ruminants, the Antilocapridae in North America.


详细解析:H段指出，pronghorn（叉角羚）是Antilocapridae亚科的唯一幸存物种。


同义替换：only living member = sole survivor
